content.problemStatement:
Given temperature in fahrenheit as input, find if weather is cold or hot. Its `HOT` out there if temperature is greater than 90090^{\text{0}}900 F, else `COLD`.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains single non-negative integer - The `temperature`.


Output Format

Output on the single line:

`HOT` if if temperature is grater than 90090^{\text{0}}900 F.
else `COLD`.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single non‑negative integer temperature (0 ≤ temperature ≤ 1 000 000 000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a line containing "HOT" if the temperature is strictly greater than 900900000, otherwise print "COLD".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Three temperatures are given. 0 and 900900000 are not greater than the threshold, so they produce COLD. 900900001 exceeds the threshold, so it produces HOT.
The single temperature equals the threshold, therefore the weather is not considered HOT.
1 is below the threshold → COLD. 1000000000 is above the threshold → HOT.

hidden.label: maximum boundary (compact)
hidden.expectedOutput:
COLD
HOT
HOT
COLD
COLD
COLD
COLD
COLD
HOT
HOT

hidden.label: mixed/stress test
hidden.expectedOutput:
COLD
COLD
HOT
COLD
HOT
COLD
COLD

solution.approach: The problem only requires checking whether each given temperature exceeds a fixed threshold (900900000). For each test case we read the temperature, compare it with the threshold and output the corresponding word. No additional data structures or preprocessing are needed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) time overall, O(1) per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space (apart from input and output storage).
